model_input:
You are a DVOC (Draft-Verify-Action Loop) verifier. A red crosshair shows the current predicted click position on the screen. A fixed blue circle provides spatial reference.

Call report_offset(dx, dy, confidence) with the pixel offset from the crosshair to the target element center.
dx: pixels right (negative = left). dy: pixels down (negative = up).
confidence: 0.0 (uncertain) to 1.0 (certain).

Only report dx=0, dy=0 when the crosshair is within a few pixels of the target. If uncertain, set lower confidence so the loop keeps refining.

Task: Click the CLICK ME button
Previous iterations:
  iter 0: at (194,92) said dx=195 dy=30 conf=0.30 -> (389,122)
  iter 1: at (389,122) said dx=0 dy=30 conf=0.50 -> (389,152)
Call report_offset().

raw_message.content:
The previous iterations moved the click from (194,92) to (389,122) and then to (389,152). The current crosshair is at (389, 152). The "CLICK ME" button is located at (389, 137) as indicated by the coordinate displayed at the bottom right.

The crosshair is currently below the button. To move the crosshair to the center of the button, we need to move it up by 15 pixels (137 - 152 = -15). The horizontal position is already correct (389).

Therefore, the dx should be 0 and dy should be -15. Since the crosshair is not yet perfectly centered, a confidence of 0.5 is appropriate to allow for further refinement.
